name: Cappuccino Mouse Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup plus 3 Tbsp granulated sugar, divided
3 Tbsp instant espresso powder or regular instant coffee
2 large egg whites, at room temperature
1/4 cup heavy cream, or as needed
recipeInstructions:
Advance Preparation: Crust can be prepared several hours in advance; it must chill for at least 30 minutes before filling. The filled pie must be refrigerated for at least 3 hours, or overnight, before serving.
Special Equipment: 9-inch pie plate, pastry brush, candy thermometer (optional)
1. Prepare the coffee base for the mousse: In a small saucepan, sprinkle gelatine over 1/4 cup cold water and set aside to soften about 3 minutes. Place over low heat and stir until gelatine is dissolved; do not boil. Remove from heat and whisk in milk, 3 Tbsp sugar, espresso or coffee powder, cocoa, cinnamon, and salt.
2. Return pan to low heat and whisk for about 3 minutes, until sugar is dissolved. Remove from heat and stir in coffee liqueur and vanilla. Pour mixture into a large heatproof bowl and set aside at room temperature.
3. Prepare the meringue: In a 1 1/2-quart saucepan, stir together remaining 1/4 cup water, remaining 1/2 cup sugar, and cream of tartar. Set over medium heat and cook, gently swirling pan several times, until sugar is dissolved. Prevent sugar crystallization by washing down pan sides with pastry brush dipped in cold water. If using a candy thermometer, clip to the pan.
4. Increase heat to medium-high and boil without stirring until thermometer reads 239-242°F, or drop forms soft ball in ice water.
5. While syrup cooks, whip egg whites in a medium bowl until medium peaks form.
6. When syrup reaches temperature, remove from heat and gradually pour over egg whites while whipping at medium-low speed. Pour slowly between sides of bowl and beaters. Continue whipping until egg whites feel cool and form stiff peaks, about 5 minutes. Set aside.
7. Prepare ice water bath with tray or two ice cubes in large bowl, water, and a few sprinkles of salt. Place coffee base in ice water bath and stir until chilled and thickens to raw egg white consistency. If too stiff, place over hot water and whisk briefly for smooth texture.
8. Whisk about 1 cup cooled meringue into coffee mixture to lighten it, then fold in remaining meringue.
9. Whip cream until soft peaks form. Fold into coffee-meringue mixture; avoid stirring hardened bits from sides.
10. Turn mousse into chilled crust and smooth top. Refrigerate for at least 3 hours, or overnight. Just before serving, arrange ring of chocolate-covered espresso beans around pie rim (optional). Yield: one 9-inch pie; 12 servings
Chocolate Crumb Crust
Process crumbled cookies into crumbs in food processor. Add remaining ingredients and pulse to blend. Turn crumbs into 9-inch pie plate and pinch together. If too dry or crumbly, add more milk. Press firmly and evenly against sides and bottom of pan. Refrigerate until firm before filling.
